en.extract:
The ashy-fronted bulbul (Pycnonotus cinereifrons) is a member of the bulbul family of passerine birds. It is endemic to Palawan in the Philippines.  Its natural habitat is tropical moist lowland forests. Until 2010, the ashy-fronted bulbul was considered as a subspecies of the olive-winged bulbul.


Description and taxonomy

It was formerly conspecific with the olive-winged bulbul but differs by its paler bill, gray-flecked forecrown, grayish white and pale belly and olive brown (instead of green) back, wings, tail and rump and different voice.


Ecology and behavior
Not much is known about its diet but it is presumed to be the typical generalist diet of fruits, insects and flowers.
Birds collected in breeding condition with enlarged gonads in April. A single nest photographed in March was  cup made with grass, plant fibers with feathers and spider silk containing one single purplish egg.


Habitat and conservation status
Its habitat is primary lowland forest and second growth. It is confined to the lowlands.
It is assessed as least-concern species under the International Union for Conservation of Nature with the population believed to be stable.  This species is able to thrive in degraded habitats and is common throughout its range.


References

Oliveros, C.H., and R.G. Moyle (2010). Origin and diversification of Philippine bulbuls. Molecular Phylogenetics and Evolution 54:822–832.

The '''ashy-fronted bulbul''' ('''''Pycnonotus cinereifrons''''') is a member of the [[bulbul]] family of [[passerine]] [[bird]]s. It is [[endemic]] to [[Palawan (island)|Palawan]] in the Philippines.  Its natural [[habitat]] is tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s. Until 2010, the ashy-fronted bulbul was considered as a subspecies of the [[olive-winged bulbul]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/updates/archives/species-2-0-2-11/|title=Species Version 2 « IOC World Bird List|website=www.worldbirdnames.org|language=en-US|access-date=2017-03-31}}</ref>

== Description and taxonomy ==
[[File:Naturalis Biodiversity Center - RMNH.AVES.126058 1 - Pycnonotus plumosus cinereifrons (Tweeddale, 1878) - Pycnonotidae - bird skin specimen.jpeg|left|thumb|A specimen from the Naturalis Biodiversity Center]]
It was formerly conspecific with the [[olive-winged bulbul]] but differs by its paler bill, gray-flecked forecrown, grayish white and pale belly and olive brown (instead of green) back, wings, tail and rump and different voice.

== Ecology and behavior ==
Not much is known about its diet but it is presumed to be the typical generalist diet of fruits, insects and flowers.

Birds collected in breeding condition with enlarged gonads in April. A single nest photographed in March was  cup made with grass, plant fibers with feathers and spider silk containing one single purplish egg.<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=del Hoyo |first1=Josep |last2=Collar |first2=Nigel |last3=Kirwan |first3=Guy M. |date=2020 |title=Ashy-fronted Bulbul (Pycnonotus cinereifrons), version 1.0 |url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/asfbul1/1.0/introduction |journal=Birds of the World |language=en |doi=10.2173/bow.asfbul1.01 |issn=2771-3105|url-access=subscription }}</ref>

== Habitat and conservation status ==
Its habitat is primary lowland forest and second growth. It is confined to the lowlands.

It is assessed as [[least-concern species]] under the [[International Union for Conservation of Nature]] with the population believed to be stable.  This species is able to thrive in degraded habitats and is common throughout its range.<ref>{{Cite report |url=http://www.iucnredlist.org/details/103834609/0 |title=Pycnonotus cinereifrons: BirdLife International: The IUCN Red List of Threatened Species 2017: e.T103834609A113108448 |last=IUCN |date=2016-10-01 |publisher=International Union for Conservation of Nature |doi=10.2305/iucn.uk.2017-1.rlts.t103834609a113108448.en |language=en}}</ref>
